$comment: Normatively defined by the AIP Endorsement Object rules. Endorsement signature covers canonical field order: (endorsement_id, from_aid, to_aid, task_id, outcome, score, notes, timestamp, signature_kid, signature) with signature set to '' during signing. Self-endorsement (from_aid == to_aid) MUST be rejected by the Registry at the application layer — JSON Schema cannot assert cross-field inequality.
title: AIP Endorsement
description: A signed task-outcome record submitted by one AIP agent to endorse another. Endorsements feed the Reputation system — they record the outcome of a task that from_aid observed to_aid perform. The Registry stores these immutably. Self-endorsement (from_aid == to_aid) is prohibited and MUST be rejected by the Registry.

endorsement_id.description: Unique identifier for this endorsement. Format convention: 'end:' prefix followed by a UUID v4. Consistent with manifest_id ('cm:') and revocation_id ('rev:') prefix conventions.

from_aid.description: AID of the endorsing agent. MUST be registered and not revoked. MUST NOT equal to_aid (self-endorsement is prohibited and rejected by the Registry).

to_aid.description: AID of the agent being endorsed. MUST be registered. MUST NOT equal from_aid.

task_id.description: Opaque task identifier linking this endorsement to a specific work unit. SHOULD match a task_id used in a delegation chain. Not validated by the Registry beyond being non-empty — the meaning is defined by the application layer.

outcome.description: Machine-readable task outcome. success — the task was completed as specified. partial — the task was partially completed (e.g. time-bounded ephemeral agent, incomplete result). failure — the task failed or was not completed satisfactorily.

score.description: Endorsement score in the range 1 through 5 inclusive. Values outside this range MUST be rejected with endorsement_invalid.

notes.description: OPTIONAL. Signed free-form human-readable audit context about the outcome. The Registry preserves this value as part of the Endorsement Object, but MUST NOT parse, classify, or use it to compute reputation scores, update counters, validate authorization, or decide endorsement acceptance. Null or absent when no additional context is needed. Maximum 512 characters.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when this endorsement was issued. MUST be a valid RFC 3339 datetime. The Registry stores this as-is; no clock skew validation is enforced on endorsements.

signature_kid.description: DID URL identifying the Ed25519 verification method used to verify signature. The DID portion MUST equal from_aid.

signature.description: Base64url-encoded EdDSA signature of this endorsement, signed with the private key identified by signature_kid. The Registry MUST verify this signature against the named public key of from_aid before recording. Invalid signatures MUST be rejected.

reputation_effect.$comment: Endorsements affect the to_aid reputation score in the Registry's reputation table. The aggregation algorithm weights endorsements by the from_aid's own reputation score, recency, and outcome severity. The Registry MAY apply a minimum from_aid reputation threshold before counting endorsements.
